title: Electric tool, method, assembly, computer program product, and computer readable medium
abstract: A power tool, in particular a hand-held power tool, for example a polishing device, a grinding device and/or a sawing device, with a tool, an electric motor for driving the tool and a control unit for controlling the electric motor. The power tool is designed for connection to a mains voltage and includes a rectifier arrangement with an intermediate circuit for providing an intermediate circuit voltage based on the mains voltage, the Intermediate circuit voltage having a plurality of successive voltage half-waves, and the control unit being configured to provide, for each voltage half-wave, a respective torque half-wave for driving the electric motor, the waveform of which torque half-wave is flattened with respect to the waveform of the voltage half-wave.
claims: 1. A power tool with a tool, an electric motor for driving the tool and a control unit for controlling the electric motor, wherein the power tool is designed for connection to a mains voltage and comprises a rectifier arrangement with an intermediate circuit for providing an intermediate circuit voltage based on the mains voltage, the intermediate circuit voltage having a plurality of successive voltage half-waves, and the control unit being configured to provide, for each voltage half-wave, a respective torque half-wave for driving the electric motor, the waveform of which torque half-wave is flattened with respect to the waveform of the voltage half-wave, wherein the control unit is configured to supply current to the electric motor in proportion to the respective torque half-wave.
